FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK DISTRICT BOARD OF
                   DIRECTORS
 ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED FIFTY-FIRST BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                              REGULAR MEETING
                     Agenda for December 17, 2025
The regular meeting of the Board of Directors on December 17, 2025 will be
                  conducted both in person and via Zoom:
                    https://us02web.zoom.us/j/87345380207
      Or join by phone: US: +1 669 900 9128 Meeting ID: 873 453 80207
                                VILLAGE HALL
                        7997 CALIFORNIA AVENUE
                            FAIR OAKS, CA 95628
                             December 17, 2025
                                  6:00 P.M.
 1. CALL TO ORDER

 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

 3. ROLL CALL

 4. PRESENTATIONS/PROCLAMATIONS/RECOGNITION

   4.I. Presentation To The Outgoing Board Chair.

 5. AUDIENCE/PUBLIC COMMENT (NON-AGENDA ITEMS)
     (Any person may address the Board upon any subject within the jurisdiction
     of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Distict. Each speaker is limited to a
     maximum of THREE (3) minutes. Any matter requiring Board action will be
     referred to staff or committee for a report and action at a subsequent
     meeting).

 6. CONSENT CALENDAR
     (All matters listed under Consent Calendar are considered by the Board of Directors to
     be routine and will be acted upon in a single motion. There will not be separate
     discussions of these items unless a request is made prior to the time the Board
     considers a motion to approve).

I, Michael J. Aho, District Administrator of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District, do
hereby certify that this agenda has been posted at 7997 California Avenue, Fair Oaks,
California and 4150 Temescal Street, Fair Oaks, California at least 72 hours prior to the
meeting of the Board of Directors in accordance with Government Code Section
54950.5, the Ralph M. Brown Act.

If you need a disability related accommodation to participate in these meetings,
please contact the Park District Office at (916) 966-1036 (voice) or (916) 966-9863

 Note: Regarding Sick-Leave hours, a Regular Full-Time employee June choose the Sick Leave payout option and
 utilize a maximum of 320 hours (as identified within the table above) which will be compensated at 50% of the
     employees current hourly rate of pay. However, upon retirement from the District, a Regular Full-Time
 employee December choose to utilize their accrued Sick Leave toward CalPERS service credit with no maximum
     limit. Employees whose Sick Leave hours exceed 320 hours are noted but the hours in excess of 320 not
calculated as a liability in the table above. A Regular Part-Time employee will be compensated for a maximum of
                   160 hours of sick leave at 50% of the employees rate of pay upon termination.

The following revision descriptions shall be included with the Village Park Renovation
project, Sheets 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 8.2, 8.3, 8.5, 8.6, dated August 3, 2023 and Sheets C1.1,

1. REVISION TO SHEET 4.0 SITE LAYOUT PLAN:
Remove approximately 43 SF of impervious pavers and 2 tree grates.
Add approximately 43 SF of decomposed granite pavement.
Add decomposed granite to the site construction legend.

2. REVISION TO SHEET 4.1 SITE LAYOUT PLAN:
Remove approximately 43 SF of impervious pavers and 2 tree grates.
Add approximately 43 SF of decomposed granite pavement.

3. REVISION TO SHEET 4.2 SITE LAYOUT PLAN:

Detail 5/8.2-Remove paver grate detail and replace with decomposed granite at
concrete detail.

5. REVISION TO SHEET 8.3 CONSTRUCTION DETAILS:
Detail 6/8.3-Revise detail to show condition in concrete or planting area.

6. REVISION TO SHEET 8.5 CONSTRUCTION DETAILS:

7. REVISION TO SHEET 8.6 CONSTRUCTION DETAILS:
Add Detail 8/8.6, showing market light in pilaster condition.

Revise the curb and gutter layout.

                                                                  DECOMPOSED GRANITE PAVING 32 15 40
                                                                          Village Park Renovation Project
                                                                                              Page 1 of 3

SECTION 32 15 40 DECOMPOSED GRANITE PAVING


PART 1 - GENERAL (See also General Provisions, Special Provisions, and General Requirements)

1.1          SCOPE

             A.            Furnish and install all decomposed granite paving as shown and specified.

             B.            Related work specified elsewhere:
                           1.     Earthwork and Grading – Section 31 01 91
                           2.     Asphalt Pavement – Section 32 12 16
                           3.     Site Concrete - Section 32 13 13

1.2          QUALITY CONTROL

             A.            Standards: Unless otherwise shown or specified, all materials and methods shall
                           conform to the appropriate current sections of:
                           1.     The State of California, Department of Transportation Standard
                                  Specifications (DTSS) Section 20-5.
                           2.     Applicable ASTM Specifications as they reasonably apply to this work.

             B.            Tolerances: Tolerances for subgrade, subbase, and finish grade shall be as
                           specified by DTSS except that Contractor shall deliver the full decomposed
                           granite thickness shown. No combination of high and low tolerances that
                           compromise the section will be permitted.

1.3          SUBMITTALS

             A.            Decomposed Granite: A one-quart sample with supplier and source clearly
                           indicated of decomposed granite to be used shall be submitted to the Owner’s
                           Representative for approval prior to delivery to the site.

             B.            Stabilizer: Stabilizer: Stabilizer shall be PHP Organic Aggregate Binder, or equal,
                           and added to the DG at the rate of 12 lbs per ton of DG.

             C.            Reviews: Contractor shall stake and layout all paving areas for review by the
                           Owner’s Representative prior to excavation.

             D.            Samples: The Contractor shall demonstrate to the satisfaction of the Owner’s
                           Representative that he or his subcontractor possesses sufficient skills and
                           experience to perform the work in all aspects required. A five-square-foot

19032_321540_DecomposedGranitePavement_SS.doc.doc
©     copyrighted 2023 Callander Associates
      Landscape Architecture, Inc.
                                                                 DECOMPOSED GRANITE PAVING 32 15 40
                                                                         Village Park Renovation Project
                                                                                             Page 2 of 3

                           sample of decomposed granite paving shall be installed at the site for review and
                           approval by the Owner’s Representative. The Contractor shall meet or exceed
                           that quality of work in all subsequent work. Contractor shall be responsible for
                           the removal of the sample at the completion of work.


PART 2 - PRODUCTS

2.1          MATERIALS

             A.            Decomposed Granite: Decomposed granite, hereafter referred to as “DG”, shall
                           be a material with a 3/8” minus gradation, per the following specifications:

                             Sieve Size                   Percent Passing
                             3/8”                         100%
                             No. 4                        85% - 95%
                             No. 8                        70% - 80%
                             No. 16                       50% - 60%
                             No. 30                       40% - 50%
                             No. 50                       25% - 35%
                             No. 100                      15% - 25%
                             No. 20                       10% - 20%

                           The yellow-brown color of decomposed granite is a requirement for this
                           material.

PART 3 - EXECUTION

3.1          INSTALLATION

             A.            Immediately prior to placing the decomposed granite, the subgrade shall be
                           moistened. The decomposed granite shall be deposited in such a manner as to
                           minimize the necessity for spotting, picking up, or otherwise shifting the
                           decomposed granite. The decomposed granite shall be leveled by raking and
                           compacted by use of a light roller.

             B.            Fill in any low spots or cracks with additional decomposed granite.

3.2          GRADES




19032_321540_DecomposedGranitePavement_SS.doc.doc
©     copyrighted 2023 Callander Associates
      Landscape Architecture, Inc.
                                                                DECOMPOSED GRANITE PAVING 32 15 40
                                                                        Village Park Renovation Project
                                                                                            Page 3 of 3

             A.            The finish grades of the decomposed granite paving shall conform to the lines
                           and grades on the drawings and allow for drainage.

3.3          CLEAN-UP

             A.            All waste produced as a result of decomposed granite paving construction shall
                           be removed from the site and disposed of legally. All excess decomposed granite
                           shall be removed from planting areas.


PART 4 - MEASUREMENT AND PAYMENT

4.1          All work under this section shall be included in the lump sum price bid for decomposed
             granite paving and shall include full compensation for furnishing all labor, materials,
             tools, equipment, and incidentals, and for doing all the work involved in decomposed
             granite paving, and related incidental work.


                                              END OF SECTION 32 15 40

                                                                    Bid Proposal

To:       Travis Nicholas                                                        November 7, 2025

Re:       Village Park Fence with a Pedestrian Gate

Scope:    1) Furnish and install 6 ft high ornamental 3 rail fence panel – 2 panels
          2) Furnish and install gate 42”x 6’ high gate with Von Duprin panic hardware



                                                                                           Price $ 13,780.00

Per plans and specifications.

Special Project Inclusions: panic hardware, expanded metals around gate.
Special Project Exclusions: Any demolition and guard rail details.

Price quoted as PREVAILING WAGE Rates

Clarifications and Standard Qualifications:
A) On site underground not located by 811/Underground Service Alert to be located and responsibility of General Contractor/Owner or others.
       Private locating treated as change order.
B)     Staking to be the responsibility of General Contractor/Owner or others. Staking includes all end, corner, fence lines and gate locations on finish
       grade.
C)     All post hole digging operations to be done with motorized equipment, i.e. Bobcat/Dandy Digger. Hand digging and potholing to be treated as
       a change order.
D) No removal of spoils from excavation. No removal of existing fences, clearing, grubbing, or grading. No temporary fence, tree fencing, ESA
       fencing, or public safety fence.
F)     No concrete gate track, mow strips, retaining walls, rebar or re-bar cages.
G) All posts in concrete flatwork, concrete walls, block walls etc. to be set before concrete pours, or blocked out by General Contractor/Owner or
       others. Post pockets and sleeves to be supplied, located and placed by others.
H) No saw cutting or core drilling.
I)     No electrical grounding or bonding.
J)     No traffic control, signs or signage, public safety, site security, public protections or barricades.
K) No access hardware, panic hardware, specialty hardware.
L)     No bond expenses, plans, permits, as built drawings, inspections, testing, engineering or associated fees.
*Project quoted to include (1) mobilization. Additional mobilizations at $ 2,200.00 each.
*All work to be done during normal business, daylight hours.
*Arrow Fence Company is not a signatory to the Laborers’ Union or any Union and will not enter into any contract where contractor is signatory to
any Union or Project Labor Agreement. *A copy of this accepted proposal is to be signed and becomes an integral part of the contract/agreement
containing terms mutually agreeable to both parties. In the event of any inconsistency between such contract and this proposal, the terms of this
proposal shall prevail. *Any additional expense out of Arrow Fence Company’s control, such as increase in material cost that includes market
instability and/or import/export tariffs and/or change in the above scope of work, will be an adjustment in the contract price in the form of a change
order. *No changes to the original quote without written consent from Arrow Fence Company. *Price quote is good for 30 days from the date above.
Thank you,

        Notes:
        Insert Contractor's basic hourly rate, health & welfare, pension, vacation/holiday and other payments in columns "D to H", then add training to column "J". Subtotal straight and over
  I     time hourly rates will be calculated at column "K" which includes "Fringes Benefits" . Please note that ONLY "basic hourly rates" will change for over time and all other "Fringe Benefits"
        remain the same as straight hourly rates.
        Insert Contractor's workman compensation rate at lower section of columns "N" where shows "0.00%" for each contractor/trade. Total hourly rate including "Fringes Benefits" and all
 II     "Burdens" will be calculated at column "R". Since "Burdens" are percentages of the "subtotal straight/OT hourly rates" they will increase for over time rates and will be calculated
        automatically by the spreadsheet.

                                        RFI #: V2 - Stage Room Fire Rated Walls
Status                   Draft

To                       Joel Rehbein (WMB Architects Inc)                        From                    Gage Burget (Bobo Construction, Inc.)
                                                                                                          9722 Kent StreetSte. A
                                                                                                          Elk Grove, California 95624

Urgency                  Urgent



Activity

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Thursday, Jan 18, 2024 at 11:31 AM PST
                          Per the fire inspector, the walls shown highlighted on the attached drawing need to be fire rated walls. We just received a
                          failed inspection due to the lack of fire rated walls. Please provide details for all fire rated wall assemblies floor to roof deck.

                          Attachments
                          RFI 397 - Stage Room Fire Rated Walls.pdf


                          Awaiting an Official Response


         WMB Response | JR | 2024-01-24
         The rated assemblies in this project are fire barriers, only being provided to satisfy the
         requirements of CBC 2019 410.4 "Dressing and Appurtenant Rooms". They are not
         otherwise being used to separate fire areas, to create fire walls, to separate
         occupancies, etc. The rated walls shown on T2.1 are the only walls requiring protection
         because they are the walls that separate the stage from appurtenant rooms. There is no
         requirement to separate the stage from exterior spaces with fire barriers or to protect
         ceilings/soffits within stages. See comments in magenta, next pg.

         Those walls are noted on T2.1, and scheduled to receive 5/8 Type X gyp brd. Install gyp
         board to bottom of floor or roof plywood above. Extent of Type X gyp bd clarified on
         attached section markup.
         WMB Supplemental Response | JR | 2024-01-29
         See attached drawings following the markups which clarify the extent of fire
         resistance rated building elements and condition at ledgers and adjacent
         construction. In general, ratings terminate at the intersection with adjacent walls and
         at the roof/floor decks above. Marked with Delta 25 "RFI 397"

           Contractor:                 Bobo                                  A/E to Owner   2/5/24
           Directed By:                Bobo
           Subject:                    FIRE BARRIERS AT STAGE
           Drawing Reference:          A2.2, A4.1,A5.1, A5.2, A6.5, A7.2,
                                       A8.1
           Spec. Reference:

Instruction:


 The purpose of this ASI is to memorialize instruction given in answer to RFI 397. See attached drawings for clarified extent
 and detail of the fire barriers at the stage. Drawings have been marked with Delta 25, issued as ASI V2-30.




 Date:         02/05/2024                         Signature:

You are authorized to proceed with this minor change in the work identified in this ASI with the understanding that no change in the
contract amount or completion date is required. If the ASI involves a change in the work affecting your contract amount or completion
date, secure Owner approval of additional cost/time prior to proceeding.

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Thursday, Aug 1, 2024 at 11:18 AM PDT
                          We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                          Clubhouse E2.1 drawing shows (5) lights at back of stage (indicaed by circles). These lights are unspecified as to what type of
                          light they are. This row of lights references note 6 which states control shown on AV drawings. There is no control indicated
                          on the AV drawings. Please provide and update to the AV and electrical drawing.

                                          RFI #: V3 - Production Lighting Support
  Status                   Draft

  To                       Tim Mattheis (WMB Architects Inc)                    From                   Gage Burget (Bobo Construction, Inc.)
                                                                                                       9722 Kent StreetSte. A
                                                                                                       Elk Grove, California 95624

  Date Initiated                                                                Due Date

  Location                 Clubhouse

  Cost Impact                                                                   Schedule Impact

  Drawing Number           E2.1                                                 Reference

  Linked Drawings

  Received From            Anders Nordmeyer (R & D Electric)

  Copies To

  Urgency                  Normal



  Activity

  Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Wednesday, Jun 26, 2024 at 02:19 PM PDT
                            We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                            Notes 1 and 6 on E2.1 do not have production rigging support or mounting details available for this location, please
                            advise.

                            Attachments
                            RFI #125 V3 - Production Lighting Support.pdf



2024-11-06 | KMMAwaiting
                 Servicesan Official Response
                                 - Robbie Wanamaker:

To support the district's request to have stage lighting available in the Clubhouse, KMM recommends the
following:

- Install a 1.5" Schedule 40 steel pipe, or "bar", be added approximately 6-8" under beam closest to the
stage.
- The bar should be able to accommodate 50lb point load, with a total distributed weight load support of
1000lb.
- Per Sheet Note 1 on sheet E2.1, intercept 2ea original circuits, install 2 quad outlets per 20amp circuit.
Distribute quad outlets evenly from each side to center to bar (total of 4 locations, 16 total outlets).
- Provide, install, and configure on the bar 6ea ETC ColorSource Spot V light fixtures with C-clamps,
safety cables, 36° fixed beam lenses, and the power/signal cables necessary for a complete and operable
system. Data sheet for this light fixture included in this response.
- Light fixture control was added to T sheets in response to RFI-466.




  Bobo Construction Inc.                                                  Page 1 of 1                              Printed On: Jun 26, 2024 02:19 PM PDT
ETC                                                                                                                ColorSource Spot V
                                                                                                                          ColorSource Series
                                                                                                                Type(s)

                                                                                                                Project
                                                                                                                  Date

                                                                                                                 Notes




G E N E R A L I N F O R M AT I O N
An LED luminaire crafted by lighting experts with a proven history of quality spot fixtures, ColorSource Spot V brings a richness of color,
increased brightness, and leading technology at a price point you’ll love. Featuring a five-color array of red, green, blue, indigo, and lime, the
ColorSource Spot V provides soft pastels and bold saturated colors from a single fixture. Featuring wireless DMX/RDM control using Multiverse®
technology, NFC configuration from your mobile device, and next level brightness, ColorSource Spot V is the ultimate in versatility.

APPLICATIONS                                                                           PRODUCT FEATURES
    • Theatres and performance spaces                                                     • Five-color array (red, green, blue, indigo, and lime)
    • Houses of worship                                                                   • Simple user interface with seven-segment display
    • Universities and schools                                                            • powerCON® TRUE1® TOP connector
    • Hospitality                                                                         • DMX/RDM in and thru (5-pin XLR)
    • Retail                                                                              • Multiverse wireless DMX/RDM
    • Exhibition centers                                                                  • NFC (near field communication) configuration
    • Meeting rooms                                                                       • LED droop compensation
    • Clubs                                                                               • Optically calibrated
    • Cafetoriums                                                                         • Tour-ready, aluminum housing
                                                                                          • Combine with available adapters to transform your fixture
                                                                                            into a fully functional Fresnel or CYC light


O R D E R I N G I N F O R M AT I O N

ColorSource Spot V Light Engine with Shutter Barrel
(For use with fixed-field lens tubes only)
 MODEL               DESCRIPTION                                                                    ETL PART NUMBER       CE PART NUMBER
 CSSPOTVMVS          ColorSource Spot V with Multiverse, with shutter barrel, black                 7423A1011             7423A1211

ColorSource Spot V Light Engine Body
(For use with zoom lens tubes and adapters and for retrofit of existing fixtures)
 MODEL               DESCRIPTION                                                                     ETL PART NUMBER      CE PART NUMBER
 CSSPOTVMV           ColorSource Spot V with Multiverse, body only, black                            7423A1010            7423A1210

Color options: -1 = white, -5 = silver gray, -8 = custom colors.
Also available without Multiverse wireless control. Contact your dealer for ordering information.
Fixture ships with a soft-focus diffuser in a gobo holder, a C-clamp, and a 1.5 m powerCON TRUE1 TOP power-input cable
with a connector of choice. See page 5 for connector options.
Please note: Lens tubes to be ordered separately. C-clamps included in North America regions only.

All LED sources experience some lessening of light output and some color shift over time. LED output will vary with thermal conditions. In individual situations, LEDs will be
used for different durations and levels. This can eventually lead to minor alterations in color performance, necessitating slight adjustments to presets, cues or programs.

Diffusers
The Soft Focus Diffuser fits into a standard A-size pattern holder and delivers beautiful, homogenized light. You can also use the Soft Focus Diffuser with patterns for dappled
and soft-edge projections.
The Smooth Wash Diffuser is used when extra-smooth blending of multiple ColorSource Spot V luminaires is required. The smooth wash diffuser is placed into the gel-frame
slot of the lens tube.

 MODEL                 DESCRIPTION                                                                                                 PART NUMBER

Activity

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Wednesday, Jun 26, 2024 at 02:19 PM PDT
                          We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                          Notes 1 and 6 on E2.1 do not have production rigging support or mounting details available for this location, please

                          V3-RFI 450 - Production Lighting Support.pdf


                          Awaiting an Official Response




Bobo Construction Inc.                                                   Page 1 of 2                               Printed On: Jan 7, 2025 11:07 AM PST
RFI #450 - V3 - Production Lighting Support                                                             Project: 3258 Village Park Renovations Project


All Replies              Response from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Tuesday, Jan 7, 2025 at 11:07 AM PST
                         We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                         The response to RFI #450 states to "Install a 1.5" Schedule 40 steel pipe, or "bar", be added approximately 6-8" under beam
                         closest to the stage. The bar should be able to accomodate 50lb point load, with a total distributed weight load support of
                         1000lb." All beams in front of the stage run perpendicular to the stage.

                         Please provide a detail drawing of how and where to install the bar. What type of anchors to use, how the bar is to be
                         seismically braced due to the suspended load, and the distance from the stage it should be mounted.

                         Attachments
                         RFI #178 V3 - RFI 450 Clarification.pdf


                         Response from Robbie Wanamaker KMM Services on Wednesday, Nov 6, 2024 at 02:54 PM PST
                         To support the district's request to have stage lighting available in the Clubhouse, KMM recommends the following:

                             • Install a 1.5" Schedule 40 steel pipe, or "bar", be added approximately 6-8" under beam closest to the stage.
                             • The bar should be able to accommodate 50lb point load, with a total distributed weight load support of 1000lb.
                             • Per Sheet Note 1 on sheet E2.1, intercept 2ea original circuits, install 2 quad outlets per 20amp circuit. Distribute quad
                               outlets evenly from each side to center of bar (total of 4 locations, 16 total outlets).
                             • Provide, install, and configure on the bar 6ea ETC ColorSource Spot V light fixtures with C-clamps, safety cables, 36°
                               fixed beam lenses, and the power/signal cables necessary for a complete and operable system. Data sheet for this
                               light fixture included in this response.
                             • Light fixture control was added to T sheets in response to RFI-466.


                         Attachments
                         V3-RFI 450 - Production Lighting Support_KMM-resp.pdf




Bobo Construction Inc.                                                 Page 2 of 2                                 Printed On: Jan 7, 2025 11:07 AM PST

  Activity

  Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Wednesday, Jun 26, 2024 at 02:19 PM PDT
                            We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                            Notes 1 and 6 on E2.1 do not have production rigging support or mounting details available for this location, please
                            advise.

                            Attachments
                            RFI #125 V3 - Production Lighting Support.pdf



2024-11-06 | KMMAwaiting
                 Servicesan Official Response
                                 - Robbie Wanamaker:

To support the district's request to have stage lighting available in the Clubhouse, KMM recommends the
following:

- Install a 1.5" Schedule 40 steel pipe, or "bar", be added approximately 6-8" under beam closest to the
stage.
- The bar should be able to accommodate 50lb point load, with a total distributed weight load support of
1000lb.
- Per Sheet Note 1 on sheet E2.1, intercept 2ea original circuits, install 2 quad outlets per 20amp circuit.
Distribute quad outlets evenly from each side to center to bar (total of 4 locations, 16 total outlets).
- Provide, install, and configure on the bar 6ea ETC ColorSource Spot V light fixtures with C-clamps,
safety cables, 36° fixed beam lenses, and the power/signal cables necessary for a complete and operable
system. Data sheet for this light fixture included in this response.
- Light fixture control was added to T sheets in response to RFI-466.

Question              Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Friday, Apr 4, 2025 at 07:27 AM PDT
                      We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                      Please provide clarification for the following in regards to the detail provided in the response to RFI #450:

                            • Please indicate the length of the schedule 40 pipe.
                            • Please indicate the number of attachments to the beam required.
                            • Sheet V3 E2.1 shows these lights approximately 5' from the front edge of the stage. The beam closest to the stage
                              where they will be mounted is approximately 20' from the front edge of the stag. Please indicate if this distance is
                              acceptable.
                            • The linear fixtures in the hall, which run perpendicular to where the beam lights will be mounted, are suspended 10"
                              from bottom of beam to top of fixture. The components in the detail, the strut plus the threaded rod hanger and clamp
                              for the pipe, are just over 10". The schedule 40 pipe would need to be mounted below the fixtures. Please indicate if
                              this is acceptable.

SUBJECT: HOOKUP EXTERIOR CONDENSATE AT TWO EXTERIOR UNITS
Per Direction of John Schmitz, we installed all necessary condensate piping, fittings, and blocks to hook up two
exterior HVAC units.

                 FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK DISTRICT
         ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED AND FIFTIETH BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                             REGULAR MEETING

                             Minutes for November 19, 2025

The one thousand four hundred and fiftieth meeting of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
Board of Directors was held on Wednesday, November 19, 2025, at the Village Hall, 7997 California
Avenue, Fair Oaks, CA.

For the Record:                     Chair Tamagni called the regular meeting to order at 6:00 PM.

Board Members Present:              Chair Tamagni, Vice-Chair O’Farrell, Director Irwin, Director
                                    Carhart, Director Mounts

Board Members Absent:               None

Staff Present:                      District Administrator Mike Aho, Administrative Assistant II
                                    Ian Roberts, Administrative Services Manager Jennifer
                                    Larkin, Accounting Supervisor Jamie Fawcett, Arts and
                                    Entertainment Manager Jennifer Schuler, Parks and Facilities
                                    Manager Sean Ventura, Recreation Manager Nick Davison,
                                    Recreation Supervisor Sabrina Bernardo

Members of the Public:              5


PRESENTATIONS:
       • Fair Oaks 2025 Chicken Festival Presentation.

DISCUSSION & ACTION #1:            Approval of Consent Calendar
A motion to approve the consent calendar was made by Director Mounts and seconded by
Director Irwin.
AYES:          Chair Tamagni, Vice-Chair O’Farrell, Director Irwin, Director Mounts
NOES:          None
ABSTAIN:       None
ABSENT:        Director Carhart
RECUSE:        None

DISCUSSION & ACTION #2:                 Discussion and Possible Action on Approval of District
Administrator Job Description.
A motion to approve the District Administrator Job Description was made by Director Irwin and
seconded by Vice-Chair O’Farrell.
AYES:        Chair Tamagni, Vice-Chair O’Farrell, Director Irwin, Director Mounts
NOES:        None
ABSTAIN:     None
ABSENT:      Director Carhart
RECUSE:      None

DISCUSSION & ACTION #3: Receive and File: Update on Concessions Agreement.
The Board received and filed the report from Arts and Entertainment Manager Jen Schuler.

         STAFF REPORT


Meeting Date: December 17, 2025

To:               Board of Directors

From:             Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:           Approval of an Agreement with CPS-HR
                   Consulting for District Administrator
                   Recruitment
I. Recommendation:
Approve the agreement with CPS HR Consulting to conduct the
executive recruitment process for the District Administrator position,
as outlined in their proposal, with authorization for staff to negotiate
minor adjustments to the scope and cost.

II. Background:
The Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District will soon begin the
process of recruiting a new District Administrator. To ensure a
comprehensive, professional, and successful recruitment, staff
recommend engaging CPS HR Consulting, a firm with over 40 years
of experience in public sector human resources and executive
recruitment. CPS HR previously assisted the District in recruiting the
current District Administrator, giving them valuable knowledge of the
District and the Fair Oaks community.
Their proposal includes a full-service recruitment process
encompassing three phases:

   •   Phase I: Development of candidate profile and recruitment
       strategy, including stakeholder meetings and a recruitment
       brochure.
   •   Phase II: Aggressive outreach and candidate identification
       through targeted advertising, database searches, and screening
       interviews.
   •   Phase III: Finalist selection process, reference checks, and
       assistance with negotiations.

The recruitment process is anticipated to take approximately 12–14
weeks from the execution of the agreement.


III. Problem /Situation/ Request:
The District Administrator position is critical to the leadership and
operations of the District. A professional recruitment process is
necessary to attract highly qualified candidates and ensure a fair and
transparent selection process. CPS HR Consulting’s experience and
prior work with the District make them uniquely qualified to assist in
this effort.
Staff requests Board approval to enter into an agreement with CPS
HR Consulting for these services, with flexibility to negotiate minor
adjustments to reduce costs slightly and retain funds for potential
candidate travel expenses during the finalist interview process.

IV. Financial Analysis:
The cost for full recruitment services (Phases I, II, and III) is a fixed
fee of $30,000, billed monthly. This amount has been budgeted for
the recruitment process, keeping the District on target to stay within
budget. Staff intends to negotiate minor adjustments to the
agreement to potentially reduce the cost slightly, allowing the District
to reserve funds for candidate travel expenses if needed. CPS HR
also provides a one-year service guarantee, offering a replacement
recruitment at no additional professional fee if the selected candidate
leaves within the first year (excluding certain conditions).


V. Summary of Key Changes

   •   Initiates a formal recruitment process for the District
       Administrator position.
   •   Engages CPS HR Consulting, which previously recruited the
       current District Administrator and is familiar with the District
       and community.
   •   Establishes a clear timeline of approximately 12–14 weeks for
       completion of the recruitment process.
   •   Includes a one-year service guarantee for candidate placement.
   •   Provides flexibility to negotiate minor adjustments to reduce
       costs and retain funds for candidate travel expenses.



Respectfully Submitted,

Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

                                           PROPOSAL

            Fair Oaks Recreation and Parks
                        District
        Executive Recruitment Services for District Administrator
                                     December 01, 2025


                                             Submitted by:
                                           Melissa Asher
                                       Chief of Client Services

                  CPS HR Consulting, 2450 Del Paso Road, Suite 220, Sacramento, CA 95834

December 01, 2025

Mike Aho, District Administrator
Fair Oaks Recreation and Parks District
4150 Temescal Street
Fair Oaks, CA 95628
Submitted via email to: maho@forpd.org
Subject: Executive Recruitment Services for District Administrator
Dear Mike,
CPS HR Consulting (CPS HR) is pleased to have the opportunity to submit a proposal to assist the
Fair Oaks Recreation and Parks District (District) with the recruitment of a new District
Administrator. We are uniquely qualified to undertake this effort as we have vast experience in
assisting public agencies with executive search, screening, and placement. We have 40 years of
experience providing human resources services to state and local government agencies and non-
profit organizations, and over 20 years of experience placing top executives in public agencies
throughout the United States.
We understand that each agency is unique, and our extensive experience allows us to tailor our
process to specifically meet your needs. Our work with local government agencies throughout
the United States gives us an in-depth understanding of government operations, programs, and
services.
CPS HR offers a broad spectrum of human resource services while delivering personalized,
results-oriented services, utilizing best practice methods of recruitment and selection strategies
from our team of recruitment experts. Each recruitment is an opportunity to shape and prepare
your organization for the future. We understand how important these transitions are and are
perfectly placed to assist you in these endeavors. Once a project begins, we will work with the
District to tailor our process to highlight the exciting opportunity and attract the best possible
candidates.
It is our commitment to work in partnership with your organization to successful results.
Thank you for the opportunity to be considered for this engagement. Should you have questions
or comments about the information presented in this proposal, please contact me at (916) 471-
3358 or via email at masher@cpshr.us.

Sincerely,



Melissa Asher
Chief of Client Services



2450 Del Paso Road, Suite 220 Sacramento, CA 95834 www.cpshr.us                    Tax ID: 68-0067209
                                                    Proposal to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
                                                             District Administrator Executive Recruitment


Scope of Work
Our proposed executive search process is designed to provide the District with the full range of
services required to ensure the ultimate selection of a new District Administrator uniquely suited
to the District's needs.



  Phase I        Develop             Phase II                             Phase III
                                                    Aggressive,
               Candidate
                                                     Proactive,
                Profile and                                                               Selection
                                                    and Robust
               Recruitment
                                                    Recruitment
                 Strategy

Phase I: Our consultant will meet with the Board to ascertain the District’s needs and ideal
candidate attributes, to target our search efforts, and maximize candidate fit with the District.
Phase II: The recruitment process is tailored to fit the District’s specific wants and needs, with
targeted advertising, combined with contacts with qualified individuals from our extensive
database and new research in the market.
Phase III: The selection process is customized for the District. CPS HR will work with the Board to
determine the process best suited to the District.

Below is a breakdown of the services included in each recruitment option.

 Task                 Description                      Outreach           Partial             Full
 Phase I - Develop Candidate Profile and Recruitment Strategy
   1    Finalize Schedule                                                    X                  X
   2    Hold Key Stakeholder Meetings                       X                X                  X
   3    Develop Candidate Profile                           X                X                  X
   4    Develop Recruitment Brochure                        X                X                  X
 Phase II – Aggressive, Proactive, and Robust Recruitment
   1    Place Ads                                           X                X                  X
   2    Identify and Contact Potential Candidates           X                X                  X
   3    Review Application Materials                                         X                  X
   4    Conduct Screening Interviews                                         X                  X
   5    Submit Client Report                                                 X                  X
   6    Client Meeting to Select Semifinalists                               X                  X
   7    Notify Candidates                                                    X                  X




                                                                                                    Page | 1
                                                  Proposal to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
                                                           District Administrator Executive Recruitment

Phase III – Selection
  1      Prepare Assessment                                                                   X
  2      Schedule Candidates; Coordinate Travel                                               X
  3      Prepare Evaluation manuals                                                           X
  4      Facilitate Finalist Selection Process                                                X
  5      Conduct Reference and Background                                                     X
         Checks
  6      Assist in Negotiation (if requested)                                                 X


Phase I:
       Meet with appropriate District stakeholder(s) to gather information on the organization
        and to develop the specifications and ideal candidate profile for the new District
        Administrator.
       Review of job duties and compensation for marketability.
       Coordinate with the Board on development of a recruitment brochure for the District
        Administrator; prepare brochure design.
       Complete recruitment brochure and post brochure on CPS HR website.

Phase II:
       Conduct aggressive outreach by initiating contact with potential applicants and with
        referral sources (this includes e-mailing/texting appropriate government management
        professionals from our database, conducting extensive outreach via LinkedIn,
        conducting research on likely qualified candidates, and contacting candidates from prior
        recruitments who may be interested and a good fit for the District Administrator’s
        position).
       Place ads in agreed upon professional and affiliate websites to attract candidates on a
        nationwide, regional, local or targeted basis based on the recruitment strategy.
       Distribute brochure/job announcement.
       Receive all resumes and acknowledge receipt thereof.
       Respond to inquiries from applicants and potential applicants.
       Keep client posted on recruitment progress.
       Review application materials and identify candidates for further consideration.
       Conduct preliminary screening interviews.
       Submit “Client Report” (includes resumes, summary of resumes, notes, overview).
       Meet with the Board to discuss the report and the results of the screening interviews in
        order for the District to identify an appropriate number to interview as finalists (or semi-
        finalists).
       Notify all applicants of status in the recruitment.



                                                                                                  Page | 2
                                                     Proposal to the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
                                                              District Administrator Executive Recruitment

 Phase III:
      Prepare an assessment process for finalists in coordination with the District.
      Schedule candidates for participation in the finalist assessment; send invitations to
       candidates and coordinate travel/accommodations.
      Prepare evaluation materials.
      Facilitate finalist assessment process in coordination with the District.
      Conduct reference and background checks on top identified candidate(s) following
       assessment process.
      Assist the District in negotiation of terms of employment (if desired).

                                                        District Administrator Executive Recruitment


Timeline
The project team CPS HR has selected is prepared to begin work upon receipt of a fully-executed
contractual agreement. All search activities up to and including the selection of a new District
Administrator can be completed in 12 to 14 weeks. The precise schedule will depend on the
placement of advertising on appropriate professional and affiliate websites, and the ability to
schedule, as quickly as possible, the initial meeting.

                                                               District Administrator Executive Recruitment


Professional Fees and Guarantee
Professional Services
Our professional fixed fee covers all CPS HR services associated with the recruitment process,
including the necessary field visits. Travel expenses for candidates who are invited forward in
the interview process are not included in the fixed fee.


                                           Professional Fixed Fees*
 Full Recruitment – Phase I, II, and III                                                       $30,000
*Professional fees would be billed and paid monthly.



One-Year Service Guarantee
If the employment of the candidate selected and appointed by the District as a result of a full
executive recruitment (Phases I, II, and III) comes to an end before the completion of the first
year of service, CPS HR will provide the District with professional services to appoint a
replacement. Professional consulting services will be provided at no cost. The District would be
responsible only for expenses such as re-advertising, consultant travel, additional background
checks, etc. This guarantee does not apply to situations in which the successful candidate is
promoted or re-assigned within the organization during the one-year period. Additionally,
should the initial recruitment efforts not result in a successful appointment, CPS HR will extend
the aggressive recruiting efforts and screen qualified candidates until an offer is made and
accepted.




  We thank you for your consideration of our proposal. We are committed to
providing high quality and expert solutions and look forward to partnering with
     the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District in this important endeavor.

           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date: December 17th, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Mike Aho, District Administrator

Subject:             Approval of Complimentary Tickets, Discounts, and
                     Group Sales Policy for Fair Oaks Performing Arts
                     Center

Prepared By:         Jen Schuler, Arts & Entertainment Manager

I.     Recommendation
       Staff recommends the Board of Directors approve the Fair Oaks
       Performing Arts Center (FOPAC) Complimentary Ticket, Discounts, and
       Group Sales Policy, effective January 1, 2026.

II.    Background
       With the opening of FOPAC and the expansion of District-produced and
       rental events, the District requires a standardized approach to
       complimentary tickets, discount programs, and group sales. Past practices
       have varied, creating inconsistencies across venues and user groups.
       A formal policy ensures clarity, transparency, and alignment with industry
       standards while supporting community access and protecting District
       revenue—particularly in limited-seating venues such as the Black Box
       Theater.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       The proposed policy:
          • Establishes clear eligibility and allotments for staff, Board members,
             artists, renters, and community partners.
          • Sets venue-specific limits based on capacity (Black Box vs.
             Amphitheatre).
          • Defines walk-up comp procedures for staff and Board members.
          • Requires all complimentary and discounted tickets to be processed
             through the ticketing system for tracking and reporting.
          •   Clarifies approval authority for exceptions or additional comp
              requests.
       This framework provides consistency across events, improves financial
       oversight, and supports operational efficiency.

IV.    Financial Analysis
       The policy has no direct fiscal impact but strengthens revenue protection
       and reporting accuracy. A $4.00 administrative fee applies to all rental-
       event complimentary tickets, and venue-specific limits help ensure
       responsible comp distribution.

Respectfully Submitted,



Michael J. Aho
District Administrator
Fair Oaks Performing Arts Center – Complimentary Ticket, Discounts, and Group
                                 Sales Policy
Adopted by: Fair Oaks Recreation & Park District

Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Applies to: Fair Oaks Performing Arts Center (FOPAC) and all District-managed venues

PURPOSE:
The purpose of this policy is to establish clear and consistent guidelines for the allocation and use of
complimentary (“comp”) tickets, discounts, and group sales for events at the Fair Oaks Performing Arts
Center (FOPAC). Complimentary tickets and discount programs are intended to support community
engagement, professional courtesy, and promotional opportunities while maintaining fiscal accountability
and equitable access.

OBJECTIVE:
This policy applies to all performances, events, and rentals held at FOPAC—including the Veterans
Memorial Amphitheatre, Black Box Theater, Community Clubhouse, and Village Park—whether produced
by the Fair Oaks Recreation & Park District (Arts & Entertainment Division) or by external renters,
presenting partners, or co-producers.

DEFINITIONS:

    •   Comp Ticket: A complimentary or no-cost admission ticket to a performance or event.

    •   Rental Event: An event presented by an external organization under an executed Facility Use
        Agreement.

    •   District-Sponsored Event: A performance or event produced or co-produced by the Fair Oaks
        Recreation & Park District.

    •   Discount Ticket: A ticket sold at a reduced rate through an approved promotion, partnership, or
        outreach initiative.

    •   Group Sale: A discounted block of 10 or more tickets sold for a single performance or event.

    •   House Seats: Tickets held from public sale to accommodate artists, volunteers, press, sponsors,
        VIPs, District oversight, and accessibility needs.

VENUE CAPACITY IMPACT ON COMP ALLOWANCES AND DISCOUNTS

Comp ticket allotments vary by venue. Because the Veterans Memorial Amphitheatre has significantly
greater capacity than the Black Box Theater, larger comp allocations may be permitted for Amphitheatre
events. The Black Box, as an intimate venue with 120–150 seats, requires more restrictive comp allowances
to protect revenue and ensure equitable access.
ELIGIBILITY & ALLOTMENTS

     •     Rental Events
           Renters may issue up to 100 complimentary tickets per event. All complimentary tickets are subject
           to a $4.00 per-ticket District fee, billed following the event.
           Renters may also provide up to four (4) complimentary tickets for District oversight, marketing, or
           operational use. Any additional agreement must be defined in the rental contract.
     •     Artists and Production Partners (District-Sponsored Events)
                 o Comp allotments vary by venue:
                           Black Box Theater: up to 4 comps per performance
                           Amphitheatre: up to 10 comps per performance
                 o The Arts & Entertainment Manager must approve additional comp requests, and this must
                     be clearly stated in the performance contract.
     •     District Staff
           Full-time, regular part-time, and part-time Fair Oaks Recreation & Park District employees are
           allotted two (2) complimentary tickets per season to District-produced events, subject to availability.
           Staff working or volunteering during a performance are not considered comp ticket holders. Eligible
           staff may also receive same-day walk-up tickets, released 5–10 minutes prior to the event when
           capacity allows. Walk-up tickets are first-come, first-served, not guaranteed, for staff use only, and
           non-transferable.*
     •     Board of Directors
            Fair Oaks Recreation & Park District Board Members are allotted two (2) complimentary tickets
           per season to District-produced events, subject to availability. Board Members attending a
           performance in an official working, volunteer, or auditing capacity are not considered comp ticket
           holders. Any additional complimentary tickets for family or guests must be purchased unless
           otherwise approved by the District Administrator. Board Members may also receive same-day
           walk-up tickets, released 5–10 minutes prior to the event when capacity allows. Walk-up tickets are
           first-come, first-served, not guaranteed, for Board Member use only, and non-transferable.*

*Standard allotment applies, but comp approval is tighter in the Black Box due to limited capacity. Amphitheatre events may permit
expanded availability if capacity allows. *

COMPLIMENTARY TICKET CATEGORIES
A. Rental Events
• The District does not automatically receive complimentary tickets for rental events.
• Renters may, at their discretion, provide up to four (4) complimentary tickets for FOPAC Management,
    marketing, or oversight purposes.
• A $4.00 per-ticket administrative fee applies to all complimentary tickets issued for rental events. There
    is a limit of 100 complimentary tickets per event.
• The Arts & Entertainment Manager must approve any additional comps beyond the 100-ticket limit
    requested by the renter, and they will also be charged the $4.00 fee per ticket.
• All comp tickets for rental events must be processed through the ticketing system to ensure proper
    tracking and invoicing.

B. Artist and Production Comps (District-Sponsored Events)
• Contracted artists or producing partners may receive between (4) and (10) complimentary tickets per
    performance, depending on the venue, unless otherwise stated in their agreement. This is venue-
    dependent; there are different limits for each venue.
• Additional comps for press, management, or family must be approved by the Arts & Entertainment
    Manager.
•   Artists may request limited press tickets for credentialed reviewers in advance.
•   Comps must not be resold or transferred for profit.

C. Staff Comps
• Full-time, regular part-time, and part-time District employees may receive up to two (2) complimentary
    tickets per season to a District-produced event, subject to availability.
• The Arts & Entertainment Manager must approve additional complimentary access for operational or
    hospitality purposes.
• Eligible staff may receive same-day walk-up tickets, released 5–10 minutes prior to the event when
    capacity allows. Walk-up tickets are first-come, first-served, not guaranteed, for staff use only, and non-
    transferable.
• All staff comps must be processed through the ticketing system for tracking.

D. Board of Directors
• Each Fair Oaks Recreation & Park District Board Member is entitled to two (2) complimentary tickets
    per season to a District-produced event, subject to availability.
• Board Members attending in an official working, volunteer, or auditing capacity are not considered
    comp ticket holders.
• Board Members should RSVP through the Arts & Entertainment Manager or Box Office in advance.
• Requests for additional guest tickets must be approved by the District Administrator and are subject to
    availability.
• Board Members may receive same-day walk-up tickets, released 5–10 minutes prior to the event when
    capacity allows. Walk-up tickets are first-come, first-served, not guaranteed, for Board Member use
    only, and non-transferable.

VENUE-SPECIFIC GUIDELINES
    A. Black Box Theater (Small Venue – Limited Comp Capacity)
       • Strict comp limits due to 120–150 seat capacity.
       • District-Sponsored Events:
           • 4 artist comps max
           • 2 staff or board comps
           • Limited pre-approved media comps.
       • Rental Events:
           • 2–4 house seats for oversight or technical staff
           • All rental comps are subject to a $4.00 per-ticket fee and must be tracked through the
               ticketing system.
       • There is a limit of (10) comp tickets per show, for staff and Board members.
       • Additional comps generally not approved unless offset by reduced allotments elsewhere.

B. Veterans Memorial Amphitheatre (Large Venue – Expanded Comp Capacity)
        • Larger comp allocations permitted due to 482 seats
        • District-Sponsored Events:
            • Artists may receive up to 10 comps
            • Staff and Board members may each receive (2) comps per event.
            • Community partners, sponsors, or volunteers may receive additional comps at the
               discretion of the Arts & Entertainment Manager and the District Administrator.
        • Rental Events:
             •     Renters may provide up to 6 complimentary tickets for District oversight or administrative
                  purposes, at no additional cost to the District.
             • The District may reserve up to 10 house seats for VIP, sponsor, or public relations use.
         •   Additional comps may be approved because venue size accommodates demand without
             significant revenue impact.

DISCOUNTS AND PROMOTIONAL PROGRAMS
•   Senior, student, and military discounts may be applied at the discretion of the Arts & Entertainment
    Manager and will be clearly listed in event promotions.
•   The District may provide reduced-price tickets to schools, nonprofits, or community partners to increase
    access to the arts. These tickets will be categorized as 'Outreach Discount' in the ticketing system.
•   Promotional discount codes may be used for marketing, sponsor benefits, or time-limited sales
    campaigns. All promo codes must be approved, created, and tracked by the Box Office Coordinator,
    with expiration dates and quantity limits clearly defined.

GROUP SALES
•   Groups purchasing ten (10) or more tickets to a single District-produced performance may qualify for a
    10–15% discount.
•   Group discounts for rental events are offered only if authorized by the renter.
•   Group reservations must be processed through the Box Office with a single payment. Final counts and
    payment are due seven (7) days before the event. Group sales are non-refundable but may be
    exchanged for another performance at the discretion of the Box Office Manager.
•   Additional discounts may be offered to educational institutions or senior groups, pending approval by
    the Arts & Entertainment Manager.

TRACKING AND REPORTING
All complimentary, discount, and group tickets must be processed through the centralized ticketing system,
using the appropriate zero-dollar ($0.00) or discounted transaction codes to ensure accurate attendance
and financial reporting. The Arts & Entertainment Manager will review and report comp and discount usage
at the close of each season.

REQUEST & APPROVAL PROCESS

Complimentary ticket requests must be made through the Box Office or the Arts & Entertainment Manager.
Allotments are based on availability and operational needs. Requests beyond standard allotment must be
approved by the Arts & Entertainment Manager or District Administrator.

TICKETING SYSTEM & TRACKING REQUIREMENTS

All complimentary tickets must be processed through the ticketing system with a zero-dollar ($0.00)
transaction. Tickets will be labeled by type and venue (e.g., Black Box – Artist, Amphitheatre – Board) to
ensure accurate attendance and revenue reporting. The Arts & Entertainment Manager will review comp
usage regularly to monitor financial impact and community benefit.

MISUSE & RESTRICTIONS

Complimentary tickets may not be resold, transferred for compensation, or used for personal gain. Abuse of
complimentary ticket privileges may result in loss of eligibility. Exceptions to this policy require written
approval from the Arts & Entertainment Manager or the District Administrator.

CONTACT INFORMATION
Arts & Entertainment Manager
Fair Oaks Performing Arts Center
4150 Temescal Street
Fair Oaks, CA 95628
916-241-3819 | 916-966-1036
jschuler@forpd.org

REVIEW & AMENDMENTS

This policy will be reviewed annually by the Fair Oaks Board of Directors. Amendments, if necessary, will be
communicated to all relevant parties.
         STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date: December 17, 2025

To:                Board of Directors

From:              Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:           Approval of Religious and Cultural Displays in
                   Parks and Public Spaces Policy

I. Recommendation:
Approve the proposed Religious Holiday Displays Policy as presented.

II. Background:
The Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District received a request this
holiday season to place a Menorah in Village Park. At the time, the
District did not have a formal policy governing religious holiday
displays in public spaces. After conducting research, the District
allowed the Menorah display; however, to eliminate confusion and
ensure fairness for future requests, it was determined that
establishing a clear policy would be the best approach moving
forward.
The Policy Committee reviewed the proposed Religious Holiday
Displays Policy on December 11 and asked for a shorter, less
repetitive version. Staff revised the draft accordingly and is now
requesting Board approval of the updated policy.
This policy will ensure compliance with applicable legal standards,
including the Establishment Clause of the First Amendment, which
prohibits government entities from endorsing or favoring any
particular religion. By adopting this policy, the District will promote
inclusivity and equal access for all community members while
providing clear guidance for staff and the public regarding
permissible displays during holiday periods.


III. Problem /Situation/ Request:
The District currently lacks a formal policy governing religious and
cultural displays in parks and public spaces. This gap became evident
when a request was made this holiday season to place a Menorah in
Plaza Park. While the District allowed the display after conducting
research, the absence of clear guidelines created uncertainty and the
potential for inconsistent or unfair treatment of future requests.
To address this, staff developed the proposed Religious and Cultural
Displays Policy, which establishes neutral, constitutionally compliant
procedures for permitting temporary, unattended displays on District
property. The policy ensures:

   •   Neutrality and Equal Treatment for all religious, cultural, and
       secular displays.
   •   Compliance with the Establishment Clause, which prohibits
       government endorsement of religion.
   •   A transparent application process requiring insurance,
       indemnification, and disclaimer signage.
   •   Clear time, place, and manner restrictions to protect safety,
       accessibility, and park operations.

At its meeting on December 11, the Policy Committee reviewed the
draft policy and directed staff to condense the document and
eliminate redundancy. Staff now requests Board approval of the
revised policy in order to ensure clarity, equity, and legal compliance
for all future display requests.

IV. Financial Analysis:
There is no additional cost to the District to implement this policy.
The process will be managed administratively as part of the District’s
normal workload and responsibilities. Any associated tasks, such as
reviewing applications and approving permits, will be absorbed within
existing staff resources.

V. Summary of Key Changes:
This is a new policy for the District. Previously, there were no formal
guidelines for religious or cultural displays in parks and public spaces.
The proposed policy introduces:

   •   A clear, neutral framework for permitting temporary,
       unattended displays.
   •   A transparent application process requiring insurance,
       indemnification, and disclaimer signage.
   •   Content-neutral criteria for review and approval, ensuring
       fairness and compliance with constitutional requirements.
   •   Defined roles and responsibilities for sponsors, including
       installation, maintenance, and removal of displays.
   •   Provisions for District-sponsored displays that reflect cultural
       diversity without endorsing any particular religion.



Respectfully Submitted,

Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

Attachments: A
Attachment A



FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK
DISTRICT
POLICY NO. ___
RELIGIOUS AND CULTURAL DISPLAYS IN PARKS
AND PUBLIC SPACES
Adopted: ____________________
Revised: ____________________




1. PURPOSE
This Policy establishes neutral, legally compliant guidelines for permitting temporary,
unattended religious and cultural displays on District property. The intent is to ensure
constitutional neutrality, provide a fair and consistent permitting process, maintain public safety
and accessibility, and distinguish between District-sponsored displays and privately sponsored
displays.




2. SCOPE
This Policy applies to all temporary, unattended physical displays installed on District-owned or
District-operated parks, facilities, plazas, or public grounds.

Displays may include religious symbols, cultural or heritage objects, seasonal imagery,
sculptures, banners, or other similar items intended for public viewing.

This Policy does not restrict personal expressive activities by individuals present on site, nor
does it supersede facility rental rules, special event permits, or other adopted District policies.




3. GENERAL PRINCIPLES
   1. Neutrality: The District shall administer this Policy without regard to the religious or
      cultural viewpoint of the applicant.
   2. Equal Access: All applicants are subject to the same criteria and process.
   3. Safety & Operations: Displays must be compatible with public use, maintenance, and
      emergency access needs.
   4. No District Endorsement: Private Displays must clearly indicate they are privately
      sponsored and not endorsed by the District.




4. PERMIT REQUIREMENTS
Applicants must submit a Religious & Cultural Display Application at least 30 days prior to
the requested installation. The application must include:

   •    Sponsor contact information
   •    Description and dimensions of the Display
   •    Proposed dates (maximum of 30 days, determined case-by-case)
   •    General requested location (final placement determined by District staff)
   •    Installation and anchoring method
   •    Proof of $1,000,000 general liability insurance, naming the District as Additional
        Insured
   •    A signed Display Agreement
   •    Agreement to install required disclaimer signage
   •    Identification of any associated ceremonies or gatherings (requires separate Special Event
        Application)




5. APPROVAL CRITERIA
A Display may be approved if:

   1.   It does not create safety hazards or obstruct accessibility.
   2.   It can be placed without damaging District property.
   3.   The dates and location do not conflict with other approved uses.
   4.   It complies with all applicable laws and District policies.
   5.   It contains no obscenity, threats, incitement, or unlawful discriminatory content.

Viewpoint neutrality applies: the District shall not deny an application based on its religious or
cultural nature.




6. SPONSOR RESPONSIBILITIES
Sponsors are responsible for:
   •   Installing and removing the Display on approved dates
   •   Maintaining the Display in clean, safe condition
   •   Conducting any needed repairs during the permitted period
   •   Keeping the surrounding area clean
   •   Ensuring the Display does not impede public use or create hazards
   •   Removing the Display promptly at the end of the permitted period

If removal is not completed on time, the District may remove the Display at the Sponsor’s
expense.




7. DISCLAIMER SIGNAGE
All Private Displays must include a conspicuous sign stating:

“This display is privately sponsored and is not endorsed by the Fair Oaks Recreation and
Park District.”

Signage must remain visible throughout the installation period.




8. DISTRICT AUTHORITY
The District may relocate, cover, or remove a Display if it becomes unsafe, obstructs operations,
is damaged, violates the Policy, or remains beyond the approved period. Immediate removal may
occur in urgent circumstances.

The District may deny future applications if a Sponsor fails to comply with this Policy or causes
property damage.




9. DISTRICT-SPONSORED DISPLAYS
The District may install District-Sponsored Displays to support community programming or
seasonal recognition. Any religious elements in District-Sponsored Displays must appear within
a broader cultural, historical, or educational context to avoid any perception of endorsement.




10. APPEALS
If an application is denied, the Sponsor may submit a written appeal within 10 business days. A
written decision will be issued within 15 business days, when practicable. The appeal decision is
final.




11. PERIODIC REVIEW
This Policy shall be reviewed at least every four years or earlier if required by law or directed
by the Board.




12. SEVERABILITY
If any part of this Policy is found invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and
effect.

Meeting Date:         December 17, 2025

To:                   Board of Directors

From:                 Michael J. Aho - District Administrator

Subject:              Revised Salary Schedule-Minimum Wage Increase
                      Effective January 1, 2026

Prepared By:          Jennifer Larkin – Administrative Services Manager


I.     Recommendation
       Approve the Revised FY 2026 Salary Schedule (Attachment A) effective January 1,
       2026.

II.    Background
       On January 1, 2026, the California minimum wage becomes $16.90 per hour. The
       percentage increase from the entry-level, first step of those positions currently
       making minimum wage is 2.4%. All other part-time positions were increased by 2.4% at
       step one. This increase was assumed in the FY25/26 Final Budget preparation for part-
       time staff and funds were included in the Final Budget in anticipation of this increase.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       Staff is recommending approval of the salary schedule for 2026.

IV.    Financial Analysis
       The minimum wage increase for part-time staff was anticipated and was included in the
       FY25/26 Final Budget approved in August.


       Respectfully Submitted,



       Michael J. Aho
       District Administrator

         MEMORANDUM

Meeting Date:        December 17, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:             Selection of Board Chair and Vice-Chair for 2026

Prepared By:         Jennifer Larkin, Administrative Services Manager

There is no formal Board Report or accompanying staff recommendation for this
item. However, a Board discussion is anticipated for the selection of the New
Board Chair and Vice-Chair for 2026.

Generally, the Board follows a rotation based on past history. The following details
the Board members who held Board Chair and Vice Chair positions each year since
2020. If the Board chooses to follow its historical process, the next chair would be
Director O’Farrell and Vice-Chair would be Director Irwin.

               2021         2022        2023       2024         2025
    Chair     O’Farrell     Irwin      Mounts     Carhart      Tamagni
 Vice-Chair    Irwin       Mounts      Carhart    Tamagni      O’Farrell



Respectfully Submitted,



Michael J. Aho
District Administrator
           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date:        December 17, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho, District Administrator

Subject:             Approval of Committee Assignments and Board
                     Meeting Schedule for 2026

Prepared By:         Jennifer Larkin, Administrative Services Manager

 I.    Recommendation
       Update committee assignments for 2026 and approve the Board meeting
       schedule for 2026.

II.    Background
       The Board has five committees which meet on a variety of topics to make
       recommendations to the full Board and make the regular Board meetings
       more efficient and effective. The committees are:

           •   Budget and Finance
           •   Capital Improvements and Construction Program
           •   Community Resources Development
           •   Management and Personnel
           •   Policy Review

       Each year the Board Chair has the option of keeping the committee
       assignments the same or making changes.

       The Board also meets the third Wednesday of every month unless there is
       a majority vote to change the date of a meeting. The Board meeting
       schedule is approved each December for the upcoming year.

III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       Both the Committee Assignments and the approval of the Board meeting
       schedule must be discussed and voted upon in the last meeting of the
       year to begin in the January meeting of the upcoming year.




Respectfully Submitted,


Michael J. Aho
District Administrator
